exercises.title: Email Template Engine
exercises.description: Build an email template system that produces properly formatted email strings. Write an `EmailTemplate` class that accepts a template string with `{{variable}}` placeholders and a `render(context)` method. Support plain text and a simple HTML version. Test with a welcome email and a password reset email.
exercises.starterCode:
import re
from datetime import date

class EmailTemplate:
    def __init__(self, subject_template, body_template):
        self.subject_template = subject_template
        self.body_template = body_template

    def _substitute(self, template, context):
        """Replace {{key}} with context[key]."""
        def replacer(match):
            key = match.group(1).strip()
            return str(context.get(key, f"{{{{ {key} }}}}"))
        return re.sub(r'\{\{(.*?)\}\}', replacer, template)

    def render(self, context):
        """
        Render the email. Returns a dict:
        {'to': ..., 'subject': ..., 'body': ..., 'from': ...}
        """
        # TODO: Substitute both subject and body using context
        # TODO: Return a dict with to, subject, body, from keys
        # 'to' comes from context['to'], 'from' is 'noreply@app.com'
        pass

    def format_for_sending(self, context):
        """Format as a complete email string (for display)."""
        email = self.render(context)
        # TODO: Return a multi-line string formatted like:
        # From: noreply@app.com
        # To: alice@example.com
        # Subject: Welcome, Alice!
        # ---
        # <body text here>
        pass

WELCOME_TEMPLATE = EmailTemplate(
    subject_template="Welcome to AppName, {{ name }}!",
    body_template="""Hi {{ name }},

Thank you for joining AppName! Your account has been created.

Username: {{ username }}
Registered: {{ date }}

Get started at: https://app.example.com

Best,
The AppName Team"""
)

PASSWORD_RESET = EmailTemplate(
    subject_template="Password Reset Request",
    body_template="""Hi {{ name }},

We received a request to reset your password.

Reset link: https://app.example.com/reset/{{ token }}
This link expires in 1 hour.

If you did not request this, ignore this email.

Best,
The Security Team"""
)

# Render welcome email
welcome_ctx = {
    "to": "alice@example.com",
    "name": "Alice",
    "username": "alice_dev",
    "date": "2024-03-14",
}
print(WELCOME_TEMPLATE.format_for_sending(welcome_ctx))
print()

# Render password reset
reset_ctx = {
    "to": "alice@example.com",
    "name": "Alice",
    "token": "abc123xyz",
}
print(PASSWORD_RESET.format_for_sending(reset_ctx))
exercises.expectedOutput:
From: noreply@app.com
To: alice@example.com
Subject: Welcome to AppName, Alice!
---
Hi Alice,

Thank you for joining AppName! Your account has been created.

Username: alice_dev
Registered: 2024-03-14

Get started at: https://app.example.com

Best,
The AppName Team

From: noreply@app.com
To: alice@example.com
Subject: Password Reset Request
---
Hi Alice,

We received a request to reset your password.

Reset link: https://app.example.com/reset/abc123xyz
This link expires in 1 hour.

If you did not request this, ignore this email.

Best,
The Security Team
hints: In format_for_sending(): email = self.render(context); build multi-line string with From:, To:, Subject:, ---, body.

exercises.title: Bulk Email Campaign Processor
exercises.description: Build a bulk email sender simulation. Write a `BulkEmailer` class that processes a list of recipients, personalizes each email from a template, handles unsubscribes, validates email addresses, and tracks send results. Print a campaign report.
exercises.starterCode:
import re

class BulkEmailer:
    def __init__(self, template_subject, template_body):
        self.template_subject = template_subject
        self.template_body = template_body
        self.unsubscribed = set()
        self.sent = []
        self.failed = []
        self.skipped = []

    def _is_valid_email(self, email):
        """Basic email validation."""
        return bool(re.match(r'^[^@]+@[^@]+\.[^@]+$', email))

    def _render(self, context):
        result = self.template_body
        for key, val in context.items():
            result = result.replace(f"{{{{ {key} }}}}", str(val))
        subject = self.template_subject
        for key, val in context.items():
            subject = subject.replace(f"{{{{ {key} }}}}", str(val))
        return subject, result

    def unsubscribe(self, email):
        self.unsubscribed.add(email.lower())

    def send_campaign(self, recipients):
        """
        Send to all recipients.
        Each recipient: {'email': str, 'name': str, 'company': str (optional)}
        """
        for recipient in recipients:
            email = recipient.get('email', '')
            # TODO: Skip if email is invalid -> add to self.failed with reason 'Invalid email'
            # TODO: Skip if unsubscribed -> add to self.skipped
            # TODO: Render the template with recipient context
            # TODO: Add to self.sent with {'email': ..., 'subject': ...}
            pass

    def report(self):
        """Print a campaign summary report."""
        total = len(self.sent) + len(self.failed) + len(self.skipped)
        print("Email Campaign Report")
        print("=" * 40)
        print(f"Total recipients: {total}")
        print(f"Sent:    {len(self.sent)}")
        print(f"Skipped: {len(self.skipped)} (unsubscribed)")
        print(f"Failed:  {len(self.failed)} (invalid)")
        print()
        print("Sent to:")
        for s in self.sent:
            print(f"  {s['email']}: {s['subject']}")

emailer = BulkEmailer(
    template_subject="{{ name }}, your monthly report is ready!",
    template_body="Hi {{ name }}, here is your report for {{ month }}."
)

emailer.unsubscribe("dave@test.com")

recipients = [
    {"email": "alice@example.com", "name": "Alice",  "month": "March"},
    {"email": "not-an-email",     "name": "Invalid", "month": "March"},
    {"email": "bob@test.com",     "name": "Bob",    "month": "March"},
    {"email": "dave@test.com",    "name": "Dave",   "month": "March"},
    {"email": "eve@company.org",  "name": "Eve",    "month": "March"},
]

emailer.send_campaign(recipients)
emailer.report()
exercises.expectedOutput:
Email Campaign Report
========================================
Total recipients: 5
Sent:    3
Skipped: 1 (unsubscribed)
Failed:  1 (invalid)

Sent to:
  alice@example.com: Alice, your monthly report is ready!
  bob@test.com: Bob, your monthly report is ready!
  eve@company.org: Eve, your monthly report is ready!
hints: Check if email is valid: if not self._is_valid_email(email): self.failed.append(...); continue
Check unsubscribed: if email.lower() in self.unsubscribed: self.skipped.append(email); continue
Append to sent: self.sent.append({'email': email, 'subject': subject})

warmup.title: Quick Recall: String replace for templates
